spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the color, background, border, outline, focus ring, and icon fill surfaces, and now compares installed-PWA accent exposure against the open-web neutral fallback.

interactive.rationale: Real color picker that recomputes AccentColorText luminance and repaints six live UI patterns; well past static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Lede promises a live grid of UI patterns updating from a simulated accent — the page delivers exactly six such patterns.

edge_cases.rationale: Includes CSS.supports() probing, live display-mode context detection, open-web fallback preview, forced-colors simulation, and an explicit black-vs-white contrast-ratio comparison for AccentColorText.

design_system.rationale: All neutral surfaces use CSS variables, focus outline overrides only outline-color (button color stays correct), no static-card pattern.

summary: The system palette explorer now distinguishes Chrome 150's installed-PWA accent exposure from the open-web neutral fallback, probes keyword support and display context, simulates forced-colors mapping, and computes AccentColorText by comparing black and white contrast ratios.
